ps140: 1, Podoben “The joy of the heavenly ranks…”

With joyful heart and firm mind, O martyr,* you boldly strove for valiant deeds in God,* undaunted by painful torments* or by a violent death.* Therefore, having suffered according to the law,* you have been radiantly crowned O Theodotus.

By the trials of your flesh, O blessed one,* you wounded the adversaries,* striking their hearts with your rebukes;* and with the drops of libations of your blood,* you utterly dried up the torrents of ungodliness,* O most blessed one.

Patiently you endured, O martyr,* being burnt with candles and having your back torn with wounds,* while crying aloud:* “Nothing shall ever separate me from the love of Christ,* neither death, nor life,* nor any other torment!”
theotokion: Having stumbled headlong through my corrupt nature,* I lie prostrate, O Virgin,* yet I flee to your serenity.* Deliver me from the storm of the adversary and from manifold temptations,* that I may unceasingly hymn your grace,* O ever-virgin Mother and Theotokos.
stavrotheotokion: Standing at the foot of the Cross of your Son and God,* and gazing upon His long-suffering, O pure Mother,* you said weeping:* “Woe is me, O my sweetest Child!* How is it that You suffer these things unjustly, O Word of God,* that You may save mankind?”
